sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with a non-healing foot ulcer on his right foot that has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer has recently become red and is draining, causing concern for the patient.
He reports having walked barefoot on the beach at Dewey Beach, which he suspects may be the cause of the ulcer.
He also mentions that the ulcer sometimes throbs and has a sensation of increased pressure when touched.
The patient's biggest concern is the redness and drainage from the ulcer.
He has a history of diabetes and has not been checking his blood sugar levels regularly.
His last A1c level was around 10.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, for which he is taking medication, but his blood sugar levels are not well-controlled.
He has not had any other significant medical conditions or treatments mentioned in the transcript.
Review of systems: The patient mentions that his diabetes is not well-controlled, but does not report any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, with no fever.
Physical exam: A one-by-two inch circular wound is present on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
There is redness, drainage, and edema present.
The wound does not have any necrosis, odor, or bony exposure.
Test Results: An x-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with signs of infection.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; suspected infection; poorly controlled diabetes; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice daily for 7 days to treat the infection.
I will perform a debridement to remove dead tissue from the wound.
I will order an ABI to determine the blood supply to the foot.
I will provide the patient with a surgical shoe to wear to reduce pressure on the foot.
The patient will return in 2 weeks for follow-up and possible additional debridement.
Follow-up: The patient will return in 2 weeks for follow-up.
